title: Digonto — The Spatial Travel OS
shortDescription: A groundbreaking operating system-inspired travel platform with custom cursor, radial menu, comparison tray, drag-and-drop trip builder, and interactive map of Bangladesh.
fullDescription: Digonto is not a website — it's a Spatial Travel Operating System. This avant-garde platform reimagines domestic travel planning as an OS experience, featuring a left dock (navigation), right control center (live stats, weather, notifications), comparison tray, radial quick-action menu, custom cursor with ring, and eight fully interactive modules. Built on a deep indigo (#1E1B4B) color scheme with amber accents, the interface uses advanced glassmorphism, animated blob backgrounds, and cinematic transitions. Modules include: Command Center (home with trending destinations, platform stats, quick links), Interactive Map (clickable division polygons with data panels), Marketplace (filterable packages with expand overlay and comparison tray), Trip Engine (drag-and-drop trip builder with live cost breakdown), DNA (story slider about the company), Blog Feed (masonry layout with infinite scroll), Support Portal (conversational chat with option buttons), and Lens (draggable polaroid gallery).
techStack: Google Fonts (Outfit, Playfair Display, JetBrains Mono)
features: 8 Fully Responsive OS-Style Modules with Cinematic Transitions
Custom Cursor with Ring and Interactive States
Animated Blob Background with 4 Layers and Noise Grain
Left Dock Navigation with Tooltips and Active Indicators
Right Control Center with Live Stats, Weather, Progress Bars, Notifications
Comparison Tray (Toggleable) for Package Comparison (Max 3 Items)
Radial Quick-Action Menu with 4 Items and Smooth Animations
Interactive SVG Map of Bangladesh with 8 Clickable Divisions
Map Data Panel with Division Statistics and Highlights
Package Expand Overlay with Detailed Information and Features
Trip Engine with Drag-and-Drop Components (12 Items) and Live Cost Breakdown
Story Slider for Company History with Navigation Dots
Blog Feed with Masonry Layout and Infinite Scroll
Conversational Chat with Option Buttons and Typing Indicator
Draggable Polaroid Gallery with Reset Functionality
Keyboard Navigation (← → arrows, 1-8 keys, Ctrl+K, Ctrl+T, ?)
Command Line Search with Autocomplete Results
Toast Notification System with Multiple Styles
Theme Toggle (Night/Day Mode) with Persistent State
designUnique: The design is unprecedented in travel websites — it functions like a desktop operating system. The left dock provides app-style navigation with tooltips, while the right control center displays live system stats. The color palette features deep indigo (#1E1B4B) as primary, amber (#F59E0B) as accent, and sand (#F8FAFC) for text. Advanced glassmorphism (backdrop-filter: blur(20px)) is applied to all panels with subtle borders. Animated blob backgrounds (4 layers) create depth with smooth floating animations, while a noise grain overlay adds texture. Typography combines Outfit (body), Playfair Display (display), and JetBrains Mono (technical elements). The interface features custom cursor interactions, cinematic module transitions (slide left/right with clip-path), and a radial menu that expands from a floating button. Every interaction is designed to feel like a spatial operating system rather than a traditional website.
developmentProcess: Built entirely with vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, implementing a custom module router with slide animations (left/right exit transitions using clip-path). Developed an interactive SVG map of Bangladesh with 8 clickable division polygons, each triggering a data panel with division-specific statistics (districts, tours, price, rating) and highlight tags. Engineered a drag-and-drop trip builder with 12 draggable components across 6 categories (Stay, Transport, Food, Experience, Safety, Essential), live cost calculation, category-based breakdown, and localStorage persistence. Created a comparison tray system that can hold up to 3 packages with add/remove functionality. Implemented a radial menu with CSS positioning (4 items at different offsets) and JavaScript toggle. Built a conversational chat system with option buttons, typing indicator, and multi-step flow. Developed a draggable polaroid gallery with mouse tracking and position reset. Added keyboard navigation (← → arrows, 1-8 shortcuts, Ctrl+K for command search, Ctrl+T for theme toggle, ? for help overlay). Implemented live counter animation (updates every 4s) and mini-charts with dynamic bar heights. The codebase exceeds 2,500 lines of custom JavaScript with comprehensive state management and event handling.

techStack: Google Fonts (Space Mono, Bebas Neue, DM Serif Display, Syne)

Live Booking Ticker with Animated Counters (3s update interval)
District Directory with Alphabetical Index and Hover Previews

designUnique: The design embraces a bold, uncompromising brutalism aesthetic rarely seen in travel platforms—heavy black borders (2-3px), monospace typography (Space Mono), commanding sans-serif headlines (Bebas Neue), and a limited color palette of sand (#F5F5DC), teal (#2D5A27), black, and white. The interface rejects rounded corners entirely, using sharp angles, bold shadows (4px 4px 0, 6px 6px 0, 8px 8px 0), and heavy borders. Each section features distinctive layout choices: the hero with split-screen design, featured cells with giant translucent numbers, the scrapbook with rotated polaroids and washi tape accents, the gazette with newspaper masthead and column layout, and the contact form with split-panel design. The aesthetic channels Bangladeshi river posters, vintage travel documents, and industrial design sensibilities.

Developed a comprehensive live booking ticker with 3-second update intervals that randomly increments counters and changes color. Engineered a district directory with alphabetical index filtering and hover preview cards (absolute-positioned images). Built a package filtering system with toggle buttons and sorting algorithms for price, duration, and rating. Created a budget estimator with real-time calculation of transport (4 options), accommodation (4 tiers), meals (৳300/day), activities (৳400/day), and guide fees (৳500/day). Implemented a contact form with validation and localStorage storage of submissions. Added magnet button effect using mousemove distance calculations (80px radius, 8px max pull). Used intersection observer for scroll-triggered 'pop-in' animations across all views.

title: Ghumantu BD - The Ultimate SPA Experience
shortDescription: A high-conversion, mobile-first Single Page Application built for Bangladesh's domestic tourism market. Engineered around three high-intent traveler personas — students, families, and corporate teams — with localized trust signals (bKash/Nagad/Rocket payment simulation, Govt Reg badge, TOAB certification), a live JS cost estimator, and 80+ distinct UI sections covering all 8 core pages.
fullDescription: Ghumantu BD is a production-grade vanilla JS SPA with a custom hash-based router managing 8 distinct views: Home, Destinations, Packages, Booking, About, Blog, Reviews, and Contact — all rendered without page reloads. The router leverages history.pushState, popstate events, and a view activation system with per-view animation resets via Intersection Observer API. The Home page leads with a fullscreen parallax hero, animated stats counter (counting up via setInterval), a featured 6-destination grid layout mixing portrait and landscape cards, special offer tiers by traveler type, a trust-building Why Us grid, video tour CTA, testimonial cards, partner logos, FAQ accordion with smooth toggle animations, and a full 4-column footer with payment icons. The Destinations page includes a live search bar filtering 18 district cards by name or region, 6 divisional filter buttons, a 4-season guide, regional food highlights, a static weather widget, travel safety checklist, packing tag cloud, cultural etiquette explainers, and a map embed placeholder. The Packages page renders 9 packages dynamically from a JS data array with real-time filter buttons by category (Group/Honeymoon/Corporate/Weekend/Budget/Luxury/Student) and a price sort dropdown, plus a full inclusions/exclusions list and a visual day-by-day itinerary timeline. The Booking view is the commercial engine — a 4-step multi-form (Destination → Preferences → Contact Info → Payment) with a persistent sticky cost estimator that recalculates in real time using destination base prices, package multipliers (0.7x for Student, 1.5x for Honeymoon, 2x for Luxury), per-night add-ons, transport premiums, and traveler count. Payment step simulates bKash/Nagad/Rocket selection with visual feedback. The About page covers Mission/Vision cards, a two-column Founder's Story with photo, a 4-person team grid, awards list, CSR activities, environmental policy, media mentions, careers board, and brand values. The Blog sidebar layout renders 5 featured articles dynamically from a JS array with category filter, tags, and a Newsletter CTA card in the sidebar. The Reviews page features a live-cycling review slider (4 testimonials with prev/next), a 5-star rating breakdown bar chart, an 8-photo hover gallery grid, and 3 local guide profile cards. Contact has a validated form (name, email, phone, subject, message with toast feedback), dual office cards, a Google Maps iframe embed, social links grid, and FAQ accordion. Design language is 'Green-Delta Materialist' — deep forest green (#1a6b3a), warm cream (#faf6ef), and antique gold (#c9a84c) with Playfair Display for editorial headers and DM Sans for modern body copy.

designUnique: The 'Green-Delta Materialist' design language draws from Bangladesh's lush landscape palette — deep emerald (#1a6b3a), warm cream (#faf6ef), and antique gold (#c9a84c). Playfair Display serif headers evoke premium editorial travel magazines, while DM Sans body text ensures readability across mobile screens. Cards use 2px border-radius for a slightly formal, trustworthy feel. All interactive elements respond with translateY hover lifts and gradient box-shadows. The hero section uses a full-bleed image with a green-to-black gradient overlay, a scrolling animation indicator, and a trust bar for payment methods. developmentProcess: Architecture began with the hash-based router, mapping 8 view IDs to DOM sections controlled by display:block/none toggling. Per-view animation resets ensure Intersection Observer re-fires on each navigation. State for the cost estimator is stored in live form values, recalculated on every input change event with a pure function (calcCost). Packages and blog posts are rendered from in-memory JS arrays using template literal HTML injection. The mobile drawer uses CSS transform transitions rather than display toggling for smooth 60fps animation. All forms use explicit JS validation with a centralized showToast() function for consistent UX feedback. The FAQ accordion uses a shared toggleFAQ() function with dynamic ID generation to support multiple FAQ instances on different pages.

title: Oitijhyo BD - The Heritage Concierge

By combining trust-signals (bKash Instant Refund, BTB Verification), real-time social proof (live booking ticker, traveler counter), and a 5-step concierge booking flow, Oitijhyo BD converts aspirational heritage travelers into confirmed bookings through frictionless UX and deep cultural resonance.
fullDescription:
Oitijhyo BD is architected as a state-driven Vanilla JavaScript SPA with a custom Observer pattern engine (subscribe/emit/set) that manages navigation state, booking form state, package tab state, and gallery filters — all without any framework dependency. The router uses hash-based deep linking with browser history API support and CSS slide-in transitions between 8 views.

Home (10 sections): Cinematic parallax hero with live traveler counter, bKash/Nagad refund guarantee banner, animated 4-step journey roadmap, 6 curated destination getaways with lazy-loaded imagery, Founder's monthly spotlight, media mentions wall, 8-region interactive Bangladesh map, cultural heritage feature grid (terracotta, muslin, Baul music), 24/7 concierge stat counters with count-up animation, and an advanced multi-column footer.

Destinations (10 sections): Full-screen search with real-time filtering, mood-based filter pills (Adventure/Relax/History/Food) using data attributes, all 64 Bangladesh district cards with lazy imagery, static weather API mockup for 4 major cities, safety heatmap with animated progress bars, local dialect guide (Sylheti/Chittagong/Khulna variants), wildlife encyclopedia (8 species), festival calendar, virtual 360° placeholder, and accessibility info.

Booking (5-step form): Step 1 (Journey Selection — destination, transport, persons, nights), Step 2 (Guest Profiling — regex-validated name/email/phone with +8801 pattern), Step 3 (Add-ons — photography, extra bed, private guide, spa, special meal with toggle state), Step 4 (Payment — promo code engine with HERITAGE20/ROYALBENGAL/OITIJHYO10 codes, bKash/Nagad/Card selection, simulated payment processing), Step 5 (Confirmation with booking reference). Real-time cost engine: (BasePrice × Persons × Days) + TransportCost - PromoDiscount.

About (10 sections): 8-event company timeline with scroll reveals, 4 expert guide bio-cards, sustainability manifesto with live stats, awards grid, CSR impact (50k trees/solar/zero plastic), partnership logos, HQ virtual tour concept, careers portal with apply flow, brand philosophy, and team values.

Travel Log (10 sections): Magazine-style featured article, blog grid, category cloud with 12 tags, regional recipe guides (Hilsa/Seven-Layer Tea/Mezbani/Shutki), gear review section, travel photography tips context, podcast player mockup with progress bar, newsletter Inner Circle signup with email validation, author social links, and a pro-traveler submissions callout.

Support (8+ sections): 3 multi-city office cards (Dhaka/Sylhet/Chittagong), searchable FAQ accordion (6 questions with smooth height animation), multi-field contact form with real-time Bangladeshi phone validation, emergency contacts grid, social media Wall of Love (6-image grid), appointment scheduler (date/time/type picker), refund info, and live chat widget.

Gallery (8 sections): 5-filter masonry grid (Nature/Heritage/Wildlife/People/Aerial), 12 lazy-loaded Unsplash images in 3-column responsive layout, 3-video testimonial carousel with play simulation, Guest of the Month spotlight, drone footage section, high-res download zone, user upload callout, and filter state management.

The Royal Bengal Dark design language uses CSS variables for #022c22 (emerald deep), #c2410c (burnt orange), #eab308 (gold) as the primary palette over #010f0a backgrounds. Playfair Display serif anchors all headings for aristocratic weight; Cormorant Garamond provides editorial body text; Space Mono grounds all UI labels in precision monospace. Noise texture overlay, glassmorphism cards with backdrop-filter, grain effect via SVG filter, and a gold gradient custom cursor complete the cinematic identity.
designUnique: The Royal Bengal Dark design language fuses deep emerald (#022c22), burnt orange (#c2410c), and 24K gold (#eab308) into a palette that echoes Bengal's natural heritage — the Sundarbans, terracotta temples, and monsoon earth. Playfair Display in black weight at 7rem creates cinematic headline drama. Glassmorphism cards with backdrop-blur float above a noise-textured dark ground. A gold-gradient custom cursor replaces the system pointer. Sticky booking ticker and floating BTB badge build instant institutional trust.
developmentProcess: The SPA was built bottom-up: first the Observer State Manager (subscribe/emit/set/setNested), then the hash router with animation orchestration, then the Intersection Observer pipeline for lazy images and scroll reveals. Each view was built as a modular render function fed by JSON data stores. The booking cost engine was implemented as a pure mathematical module (BookingEngine.calculate()) subscribed to bookingData state changes, rendering live breakdowns on every input event. All 2500+ lines follow a consistent template-function pattern — no innerHTML strings over 50 lines, every section encapsulated.

title: Ondhakarer Alo - Immersive Travel Experience

title: Apex Horizon — Ultra-Luxury Concierge SPA
shortDescription: A cinematic, dark-mode SPA engineered for high-net-worth travelers seeking bespoke expedition experiences. Every UI decision — from the Cormorant Garamond typography to the gold-on-obsidian palette — communicates scarcity, discretion, and extreme quality. Built for an audience that measures experiences in exclusivity, not affordability.
fullDescription: Apex Horizon is a 2,100+ line framework-less SPA built on a custom Vanilla JS hash router that manages five distinct views: Discover, Expeditions, Private Jet, Concierge, and Editorial. The router performs a GSAP cross-fade transition between views and critically calls `ScrollTrigger.getAll().forEach(t => t.kill())` on every navigation event, destroying all registered GSAP ScrollTrigger instances before re-initializing them in the incoming view — preventing the memory leaks and ghost animations that plague long-running SPAs. GSAP 3.12 drives every major animation: the hero entrance uses a staggered `gsap.from()` call with `power3.out` easing; the custom cursor implements a requestAnimationFrame loop with linear interpolation (lerp at 0.12 factor) for the trailing ring, creating the signature luxury cursor feel. The dark-mode Tailwind architecture uses CSS custom properties (`--obsidian`, `--gold`, `--graphite`) as the foundational palette, never relying on Tailwind utility classes for color, ensuring perfect consistency. The 'Dark Elegance' design language pairs Cormorant Garamond (a refined Garamond revival with exquisite italic) for display type with Raleway for interface labels and EB Garamond for body text — a three-font system that mirrors how luxury print editorial is typeset. The Expeditions view features true horizontal scroll with CSS `scroll-snap-type: x mandatory` and custom thin scrollbar styling. The Private Jet view includes a JavaScript fleet switcher that shows/hides slides by toggling `display:grid`. The Editorial page implements a dynamic reading-time calculator that estimates article length based on excerpt word count. The four-step Concierge inquiry form manages state through a `conciergeStep` integer, rendering destination-toggle pills (using a JavaScript Set for selection state), style preference checkboxes, a generated mini-calendar with click selection, and final NDA acknowledgment — all validated before a toast confirmation. 50+ real Unsplash images are woven throughout, each filtered with CSS `brightness()` and `saturate()` to maintain the moody, editorial atmosphere.
techStack: Google Fonts (Cormorant Garamond, Raleway, EB Garamond)
features: Seamless Cross-fade SPA Routing with GSAP
Memory-Leak Prevention — ScrollTrigger.kill() on route change
Custom RAF Cursor with Gold Lerp-Trailing Ring
Horizontal Scroll Gallery with CSS snap
4-Step Bespoke Concierge Inquiry with JS State
Mini Calendar UI with day selection
Live Global Timezone Footer (4 cities, auto-updates)
Masonry Editorial Grid with Category Filter
Film Grain Texture Overlay via SVG filter
designUnique: The 'Cinematic Minimalist' language is built on restraint as a statement. The obsidian (#080a0d) base is darker than typical dark themes — it absorbs light rather than reflecting it, creating a sense of depth and gravitas. The gold (#c9a96e) accent is antique, not metallic — warm and aged rather than flashy. Typography pairs Cormorant Garamond (which carries centuries of French fine publishing history) with EB Garamond for body text — both rooted in classical serif tradition, signaling permanence in a world of trendy sans-serifs. The grain texture overlay across the entire viewport adds analogue warmth. Whitespace is used architecturally: sections breathe, content is never rushed. The custom cursor — a 6px gold dot with a 36px expanding ring — transforms every hover into a tactile interaction.
developmentProcess: The primary engineering challenge was GSAP lifecycle management inside a stateless router. Each view re-initializes its own ScrollTrigger instances, but without explicit cleanup, old triggers continue firing on DOM elements in hidden views, causing performance degradation and visual glitches. The solution was a centralized `scrollTriggers` array that collects all created instances, then calls `.kill()` on every member before each navigation. The four-step concierge form required careful state isolation: each step is rendered as a separate DOM section shown/hidden via `display` toggling, with a `conciergeStep` integer as the single source of truth. The destination pills use a JavaScript `Set` object for O(1) selection/deselection, avoiding array manipulation overhead. The fleet switcher required CSS `display:grid` toggling rather than opacity to prevent layout collapse in the hidden slides.

developmentProcess: প্রথমে ১২টি পৃষ্ঠার একটি স্ট্রাকচার তৈরি করা হয়। প্রতিটি পৃষ্ঠার জন্য অভিন্ন নেভিগেশন ও ফুটার ডিজাইন করা হয়। তারপর ধাপে ধাপে প্রতিটি পৃষ্ঠা তৈরি করা হয় - হোম পেজ থেকে শুরু করে বুকিং পেজ পর্যন্ত। JavaScript ব্যবহার করে সব ইন্টারঅ্যাকটিভ ফিচার ইমপ্লিমেন্ট করা হয় যেমন ট্যাব সুইচিং, ফর্ম ভ্যালিডেশন, প্রাইস ক্যালকুলেটর, সার্চ ফাংশনালিটি, ফিল্টারিং সিস্টেম ইত্যাদি। প্রতিটি ফর্ম (কন্টাক্ট ফর্ম, বুকিং ফর্ম, জব অ্যাপ্লিকেশন ফর্ম) সম্পূর্ণ ফাংশনাল এবং ভ্যালিডেশন সহ তৈরি করা হয়েছে। Flatpickr লাইব্রেরি ব্যবহার করে বাংলা ভাষায় ডেট পিকার ইমপ্লিমেন্ট করা হয়েছে। Leaflet ব্যবহার করে ইন্টারঅ্যাকটিভ ম্যাপ যুক্ত করা হয়েছে। সবশেষে, প্রতিটি পৃষ্ঠার লিংক ও বাটন পরীক্ষা করে নিশ্চিত করা হয়েছে যে সবকিছু সঠিকভাবে কাজ করছে।
